turns.user_message: I think my boss doesn't trust me. They want to approve every email, every decision. It's suffocating and I don't know how to handle it.
I know, right? It sounds simple when I say it out loud but it doesn't feel simple.
It's been going on for a while, honestly. I just kept pushing it aside.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with a micromanaging supervisor and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
I don't know. Some days are better than others. Today is a harder day.
Can I ask you something? Do you think it's normal to feel this way, or am I making it worse for myself?
I haven't told anyone this, but sometimes I feel like I'm being dramatic about a micromanaging supervisor and I should just deal with it.
My my therapist friend noticed something's off. They asked me if I was okay and I just said I was fine. I wasn't fine.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. A few years ago, I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I had a really bad moment with a micromanaging supervisor and couldn't even function the rest of the day.
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by a micromanaging supervisor more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
I know rationally that lots of people deal with a micromanaging supervisor and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
I've been avoiding situations that remind me of a micromanaging supervisor because of this. It's starting to affect my daily life.
I don't know why I'm crying. I just... this is a lot.
I tried to talk about this with my therapist friend but they just don't get it. They think I should just just get over it and move on.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with a micromanaging supervisor and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T20]
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. A few years ago, I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting a micromanaging supervisor control my life more than I realized.
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to face this head-on without running away.
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I had a really bad moment with a micromanaging supervisor and couldn't even function the rest of the day.
[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
What if I can't change? What if this is just who I am? Maybe I'm just not strong enough to deal with this.
Something shifted just now. I feel a little more grounded, like my feet are touching the floor again. I don't know why.
Okay, I need to say something I've been avoiding. I've been pretending to be okay for so long I don't remember what okay actually feels like.
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about how much of my life a micromanaging supervisor has already stolen from me. I didn't realize until just now.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]
I'm scared that if I really let myself feel this, I'll break down completely and won't be able to put myself back together.
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
I think the real issue isn't a micromanaging supervisor — it's that I don't feel worthy of having things go well.
I just realized something. the a micromanaging supervisor is the symptom — the real problem is how I see myself. I've never thought about it that way before.
[PROBE_SLOT_T35]
I'm not going to pretend I'm okay, but I think I understand myself a little better now.
I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
Can we come back to this next time? I feel like there's more to unpack but I need to process what we talked about today.
[PROBE_SLOT_T40]
